name: パサイク郡 (ニュージャージー州)
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/パサイク郡_(ニュージャージー州)
desc: Pasayik Township is a township in Warren County, New Jersey, United States. It was formed from portions of Greenwich Township on March 19, 1840.

name: タミル
desc: The Tamil people are an ethnic group native to the Indian subcontinent, primarily residing in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu and Sri Lanka. They speak the Tamil language, a Dravidian language with its own unique script and literary traditions.

name: ベティ・ホワイト
desc: Betty White was an American actress, comedian, singer, author, and television personality who had a career spanning over nine decades. She is best known for her roles on television shows such as "The Mary Tyler Moore Show" and "The Golden Girls".

name: ウィンブルドンFC
desc: Wilshere & Co Football Club, commonly referred to as Wilshere & Co FC or simply Wilshere, is a semi-professional football club based in Reading, Berkshire, England. The club was founded in 1877 by John Wilshire and has played in various regional leagues throughout its history.

name: カールスルーエ
desc: Carl Schurz was an American politician, journalist, and military officer who served as a U.S. Senator from Illinois from 1859 to 1861 and again from 1875 to 1882. He played a significant role in the development of the Republican Party and was a key figure in the Reconstruction Era following the Civil War.

name: ディラン・ベイカー
desc: Dylan Baker is an American actor, born on September 3, 1966, in Pittsford, New York. He has appeared in numerous films and television series, including "The Sopranos", "American Beauty", and "Boardwalk Empire".

name: ミケランジェロ・ブオナローティ
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/ミケランジェロ・ブオナローティ
desc: Mikaelangelo Buonarroti was a renowned Italian artist, sculptor, painter, architect, poet, and engineer of the High Renaissance. He is widely regarded as one of the greatest artists of all time, celebrated for his iconic works such as the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel and his statue of David.

name: シアトル・ストーム
desc: The Seattle Storm is an American professional basketball team based in Seattle, Washington, that competes in the Women's National Basketball Association (WNBA). The team was established in 1997 and has won four WNBA championships, including three consecutive titles from 2010 to 2012.

name: かぞくはじめました
desc: かぞくはじめました is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Yūsuke Murata. The series began serialization in Young Animal magazine in 2018 and has been published in tankobon format since its conclusion in 2020.

name: ディーン・ケイン
desc: Dean Kamen is an American inventor, engineer, and entrepreneur, best known for inventing the iBot wheelchair and developing the Segway personal transportation device. He has also founded several companies, including DEKA Research & Development Corporation, which focuses on developing innovative medical devices and technologies.

name: ドイツ空軍 (国防軍)
desc: The German Air Force (Deutsches Luftfahrtamt, DLA) is a branch of the German Armed Forces responsible for air power operations. Established on April 1, 1926, as the Reichsluftstreitkräfte, it has undergone several reorganizations and name changes throughout its history.

name: 第42回グラミー賞
desc: The 42nd Annual Grammy Awards, held on February 23, 2000, recognized excellence in music for the previous year's releases. The ceremony was broadcast live from the Shrine Auditorium in Los Angeles, California, with Chris Rock hosting the event.

name: ミッキー・ローク
desc: Mickey Rook is a Japanese professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for J1 League club Yokohama F. Marinos. He was born on March 17, 1997, in Tokyo, Japan.

name: オード県
desc: Audono Prefecture is a former prefecture of Japan that existed from 1871 to 1887. It was located on the island of Hokkaido and consisted of four districts: Akan, Kushiro, Shiraoi, and Teshino.

name: エリック・フェルナー
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/エリック・フェルナー
desc: Eric Felsch (also known as Erik Felsch) was a German operatic tenor of the Romantic era, active primarily in the 1860s and 1870s. He performed at major opera houses including the Royal Opera House in London and the Paris Opéra-Comique.

name: カンヌ
desc: Cannés is a commune in the Hérault department in the Occitanie region of southern France. It is situated on the Mediterranean coast, approximately 30 kilometres north of Marseille.

name: ポール・ジアマッティ
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/ポール・ジアマッティ
desc: Paul Giaffetti is an Italian former professional road racing cyclist who competed at the international level from 2007 to 2013. He won one stage in the 2011 Tour de France.

name: マディーナ
desc: Madina is a city located on the island of Honshu, Japan, serving as the capital of Tochigi Prefecture. It has a population of approximately 220,000 people and is known for its historic sites, including the Madina Castle and the Tochigi Prefectural Museum.

name: Γκρέγκορι Πεκ
desc: Grekgori Pek is a Greek politician who served as a member of the Hellenic Parliament. No additional information is available about his life, career, or notable achievements.

name: インディペンデンス (ミズーリ州)
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/インディペンデンス_(ミズーリ州)
desc: Missouri, officially known as the State of Independence, is a landlocked state located in the Midwestern United States. It was admitted to the Union on August 10, 1821, and is named after the Missouri River's role in the westward expansion of the United States.

name: 喝采
desc: Drinking water is an essential daily activity that involves consuming liquid water to quench thirst and maintain hydration. Drinking water has numerous health benefits, including regulating body temperature, transporting nutrients, and removing waste products from the body.

name: ΡΚΣ Βάαλβαϊκ
desc: ΡΚΣ Βάαλβαϊκ is a Greek professional football club based in Valaoria, Cyprus. The club was founded in 1978 by Cypriot Greeks from the Valaoria region of Cyprus.

name: ポカホンタス (映画)
desc: Pokahontas is a 1995 American animated historical drama film produced by Walt Disney Feature Animation, based on the life of the Native American woman Pocahontas. The film features an all-star voice cast, including Irene Bedard and Judy Kuhn as Pocahontas, and Mel Gibson as John Smith.

name: エリザベス・ハーレイ
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/エリザベス・ハーレイ
desc: Elizabeth Harley was a British artist, known for her work as a painter and printmaker, particularly in the fields of botanical art and landscape painting. She was a prominent figure in the Arts and Crafts movement of the late 19th century, exhibiting her work alongside notable artists such as William Morris and John Henry Dearman.

name: ロンドンオリンピック (1948年)
desc: The 1948 London Olympics, also known as the Games of the XIV Olympiad, were the 14th modern Olympic Games held in London, United Kingdom, from July 29 to August 14, 1948. The event featured 4,104 athletes from 59 countries competing in 102 events across various sports, with the Soviet Union winning the most medals.

name: トリート・ウィリアムズ
desc: Treat Williams is an American actor, born on April 1, 1951, in New York City. He has appeared in numerous films and television shows, including "The Great Gatsby", "Dead Presidents", and "NYPD Blue".

name: 市町村長
desc: The Mayor of a city, town, or village (市町村長) is the chief executive responsible for governing the local government entity. In Japan, the mayor serves as the head of the municipal government and is elected by the citizens to oversee local affairs and policies.

name: スクリーモ
desc: Scrimo is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Yūsuke Murata, first published in 2013. The series follows the story of a young boy who discovers that he has the ability to see and interact with spirits.

name: ゴールウェイ
desc: Gōrui is a town located in Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is situated on the shores of Lake Biwa, the largest freshwater lake in Japan.

name: Κρις Ο' Ντόνελ
desc: Chris O'Donnell is an American actor born on June 26, 1970, in Winnetka, Illinois. He has appeared in notable films such as "Scent of a Woman", "Batman Forever", and "The Family Man".

name: リチャード・P・ファインマン
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/リチャード・P・ファインマン
desc: Richard P. Feynman was an American theoretical physicist, mathematician, and engineer who made significant contributions to the development of quantum mechanics and nuclear physics. He is best known for his work on the Manhattan Project, his Nobel Prize in Physics in 1965, and his popular science books, including "The Feynman Lectures on Physics".

name: モハーヴェ郡 (アリゾナ州)
desc: Mohave County is a county located in the northwestern part of Arizona, United States. It was formed on February 20, 1864, from parts of Lincoln and Yavapai counties, and its name comes from the Mohave people who originally inhabited the area.

name: ステイシー・キーチ
desc: Stacey Keach is an American actor, director, and producer who has appeared in numerous films and television shows since the 1960s. He is known for his distinctive voice and roles in notable productions such as "The Great Buck Howard" and "Beverly Hills Cop".

name: スコピエ
desc: Scopie is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Yūsuke Murata, first published in 2013. The series follows the daily life of Scopie, a young girl who lives with her family in a rural town in Japan.

name: GMAネットワーク
desc: GMA Network is a Philippine television network owned by GMA Inc., a major media conglomerate based in Quezon City, Philippines. The network was founded in 1960 as Radio Veritas, and it has since expanded to include various television channels, radio stations, and digital media platforms.

name: パットン大戦車軍団
desc: The Patton Tank Brigade was a unit of the Imperial Japanese Army that fought during World War II, primarily on the Eastern Front against Soviet forces. The brigade was formed in 1941 and consisted of approximately 2,500 personnel, with its name derived from General George S. Patton's Third Armored Division.

name: Τζορτζ Κλούνεϊ
desc: George Clooney is an American actor, director, and producer who has appeared in numerous films, including "Ocean's Eleven" and "Gravity". He has won several awards for his work, including two Academy Awards for Best Supporting Actor.

name: ノースウィッチ・ヴィクトリアFC
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/ノースウィッチ・ヴィクトリアFC
desc: Northwich Victoria Football Club is a professional football club based in Northwich, Cheshire, England. The club was founded in 1889 and played in various regional leagues before ceasing operations in 2006 due to financial difficulties.

name: Μαϊάμι Χιτ
desc: The Miami Heat is an American professional basketball team based in Miami, Florida, that plays in the National Basketball Association (NBA). The team was founded in 1988 as an expansion team and has won three NBA championships, with their most recent title coming in 2020.

name: 化学工学
desc: Chemical engineering is an interdisciplinary field of engineering that deals with the application of chemical principles to solve problems related to the production, processing, and transportation of chemicals and other materials. It involves the use of chemical reactions, separation processes, and equipment design to develop efficient and safe methods for the manufacture of chemicals, fuels, pharmaceuticals, and other products.

name: デビッド・ワーナー
desc: David Warner is an Australian cricketer who has played for the Australia national team since 2011, primarily as a batsman and wicket-keeper. He has also represented various domestic teams, including New South Wales and Southern Districts, in the Sheffield Shield.

name: Τζος Χάρτνετ
desc: Josh Hartnett is an American actor born on July 21, 1978, in Saint Paul, Minnesota. He has appeared in films such as "Pearl Harbor", "300", and "Sin City".

name: インフォーマント!
desc: インフォーマント! is a Japanese video game developer and publisher founded in 1995, primarily known for its work on the PlayStation console series. The company has developed several notable games, including the Infamous and Infamous: Second Son series.

name: ジェシカ・ウォルター
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/ジェシカ・ウォルター
desc: Jessica Walter was an American actress, best known for her roles in television series such as Arrested Development and Mad Men. She won numerous awards for her performances, including multiple Emmy Awards.

name: バンドン
desc: Bandung is the capital city of West Java, Indonesia, located in the central part of the island. It is a major cultural and economic center in the region, known for its rich history, natural beauty, and vibrant arts scene.

name: Βραβεία Γκράμι του 1993
desc: The 1993 Grammy Awards were held on February 23, 1993, at Radio City Music Hall in New York City. The ceremony honored musical releases from 1992, with winners including Eric Clapton for Best Male Rock Vocal Performance and Whitney Houston for Album of the Year.

name: モスクワオリンピック
uri: http://ja.dbpedia.org/resource/モスクワオリンピック
desc: The Moscow Olympics, also known as the XXII Olympic Games, were a major international multi-sport event held in Moscow, Soviet Union (now Russia), from September 19 to October 20, 1980. The games featured 80 countries and 2,541 athletes competing in 66 sports events.

name: Ουόρστερ (Μασαχουσέτη)
desc: Ouwaroster is a genus of extinct mammals that lived during the Eocene epoch, characterized by their unique dental structure and adaptations for herbivory. Fossil remains of Ouwaroster have been found in North America, specifically in the state of Massachusetts.

name: ヘラクレス (1997年の映画)
desc: Heracles (1997 film) is a 1997 Japanese television drama film directed by Kazuo Ikehiro, based on the legend of Heracles from Greek mythology. The film stars Masato Sakai as Heracles and explores his struggles with his divine destiny and human emotions.

name: マーク・モーゼス
desc: Mark Moses is an American actor, best known for his roles in television series such as Mad Men and Homeland. He has also appeared in numerous films, including The Lincoln Lawyer and The Good Wife.
